channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sydney Airport launches paid priority security access for international flights
item.description: Sydney Airport has introduced a paid priority pass system allowing international travellers to bypass standard security and immigration queues. For $20, passengers can purchase a Fast Track pass in ad...

item.title: Kenyan Female Rally Driver Recognized for Excellence in Motorsport
item.description: Pauline Shegu, a prominent female rally driver in Kenya, has been nominated for the Outstanding Sports Personality award at the third edition of the International Women Parliament. This recognition hi...

item.title: US-Iran tensions drive oil prices higher amid Red Sea closure threats
item.description: Oil prices climbed on Friday as escalating tensions between the US and Iran disrupted Gulf operations, with the Strait of Hormuz facing reduced oil flows due to a broken truce. Brent crude futures ros...

item.title: Governor Mutfwang Reinstates Plateau Monarch, Boosts Traditional Leadership
item.description: Governor Caleb Manasseh Mutfwang has reinstated Alhaji Abdullahi Idris as the Rekna of Bashar, the highest traditional ruler in the district, after his suspension in 2024. The Bashar Chiefdom Communit...

item.title: 60 US Senators Propose 100% Tariffs on India for Purchasing Russian Oil
item.description: A new bill backed by 60 US Senators proposes imposing up to 100% tariffs on India and four other nations for purchasing Russian oil. The Sanctioning Russia Act of 2026, supported by President Donald T...

item.title: OAS Pension Rules Prevent Double Dipping for Retirees
item.description: The article explains that the Old Age Security (OAS) pension rules in Canada prevent retirees from benefiting from both residency bonuses and deferral bonuses simultaneously. A person who qualifies fo...

item.title: Namibian Citizens Repatriated from South Africa Seek Return Amid Xenophobic Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the repatriation of 72 Namibians from South Africa following xenophobic attacks, with many expressing hope to return once tensions subside. The Namibian government has stated it ...

item.title: Émile Vernon: Romantic French Artist of the Belle Époque
item.description: Émile Vernon (1872–1919) was a French artist renowned for his delicate and romantic style during the Belle Époque period. His works, primarily created in watercolor and gouache, captured the elegance ...

item.title: Wildlife Behavior Varies Based on Human Threat Type
item.description: A study conducted by researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) reveals that animals exhibit distinct behavioral responses to different types of human threats. The research highlights how s...

item.title: Peter Obi Urges EFCC to Prioritize Major Corruption Cases Over Social Media Probes
item.description: The Presidential Candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has called on the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) to refocus its efforts on major corruption cases rathe...

item.title: Economic Malaise and Political Inaction: A Nation in Stagnation
item.description: The article highlights the growing economic challenges facing Australia, with the past 10 weeks revealing a nation struggling with stagnation and declining productivity. Politicians are urged to addre...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Leadership Transition and Policy Commitments
item.description: The article details Andy Burnham's ascension as the new leader of the Labour Party, emphasizing his pledge to prioritize 'unashamedly Labour' policies. It highlights his plans for the party's future, ...

item.title: Unifying the Sea Peoples: Tracing the Bronze Age collapse through ancient texts and archaeology
item.description: The article explores the enigmatic 'Sea Peoples' mentioned in ancient texts and their potential role in the collapse of the Bronze Age world. While often blamed for the downfall of civilizations like ...

item.title: Fourth Suspect in American Scientist's Killing Surrenders
item.description: A fourth suspect in the murder case of an American scientist has surrendered to authorities, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. The incident, which occurred in Negros Oriental, has drawn significa...

item.title: Missing Calgary Boy Last Seen at Cambrian Heights Day Home: Police Search Ongoing
item.description: A 12-year-old boy from Calgary has been reported missing after last being seen at a day home in the Cambrian Heights neighborhood on July 16, 2026. Police have launched an investigation to locate the ...

item.title: Mother Arranges Introduction Without Warning Sparks Social Media Reaction
item.description: A Gen Z woman received a WhatsApp message from an unknown man who claimed her mother had given him her number while riding a bus. The stranger's reply, revealing the source of her contact information,...

item.title: Adaptation Over Strength: Lessons from Zinj Day
item.description: The article discusses the significance of Zinj Day, commemorating the 1959 discovery of Zinjanthropus boisei fossils at Olduvai Gorge. It highlights how physical strength alone did not ensure survival...

item.title: Exploring Japanese Retro-Futurism of the 1960s: Nostalgia Meets Visionary Design
item.description: The article delves into the unique allure of Japanese retro-futurism from the 1960s, highlighting how it merges nostalgia with visionary design elements distinct from Western counterparts. Unlike West...

item.title: Kaduna State Launches Comprehensive Health Initiatives to Combat Malnutrition
item.description: The Kaduna State government has pledged to strengthen its efforts in combating malnutrition through targeted health interventions. Officials highlighted key metrics such as Vitamin A supplementation c...

item.title: Japan Adjusts Imperial Rules, Yet Women Remain Excluded from Throne
item.description: Japan's imperial rules have undergone minor adjustments, allowing female members of the royal family to potentially ascend the throne under specific conditions. However, the core principle of male-onl...
